memoryArtifacts.description: Coat of arms of Bernard De La Tour. With de La Tour d'Auvergne shield symbol, and Saint-Omer and Adhémar + sign symbols. With French noble yellow lily on blue symbol. With Beaumont yellow French lily arrangment.
Coat of arms of la Tour D'Auvergne. Showing ancestry or family relationship with the de La Tour, Billung von Sachsen and Counts of Toulouse families.
Cot of arms of d'Auvergne-Bernard De La Tour.
Coat of arms of Counts of Toulouse. Toulouse taken from the Visigoths, together with all of Aquitaine by the Franks. https://db0nus869y26v.cloudfront.net/it/Contea_di_Tolosa .

zh.lifeSummary: 當Bertrand II de LA TOUR-d'AUVERGNE於大約1135年出生在法國奧維-o-阿爾培斯普-德-o拉 圖-達奧維時，他的父親Bertrand Ier de La Tour56歲，母親Métheline de BEZIERS-SAUVIAN46歲。他和Judith de La Tour de Mercoeur至少有3個兒子。他去世於大約1213年享年80歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: LA
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Bertrand
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval French variant of   Bertram  , imported to Britain by the Normans. In modern times it has been made famous by the English philosopher  Bertrand Russell  (  1872–1970 ), but is only in occasional use.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Bertrand II de LA TOUR-d'AUVERGNE was born about 1135, in La Tour-d'Auvergne, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France, his father, Bertrand Ier de La Tour, was 56 and his mother, Métheline de BEZIERS-SAUVIAN, was 46. He had at least 3 sons with Judith de La Tour de Mercoeur. He died about 1213, at the age of 80.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Bertrand II de LA TOUR-d'AUVERGNE est né environ 1135, à La Tour-d'Auvergne, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France, son père, Bertrand Ier de La Tour, avait 56 ans et sa mère, Métheline de BEZIERS-SAUVIAN, avait 46 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils avec Judith de La Tour de Mercoeur. Il est décédé environ 1213, à l’âge de 80 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Bertrand II de LA TOUR-d'AUVERGNE um 1135 in La Tour-d'Auvergne, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Bertrand Ier de La Tour 56 und seine Mutter Métheline de BEZIERS-SAUVIAN 46. Er hatte mit Judith de La Tour de Mercoeur mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb um 1213 im Alter von 80 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Bertrand II de LA TOUR-d'AUVERGNE è nato il circa 1135 nel La Tour-d'Auvergne, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia, il suo padre, Bertrand Ier de La Tour, aveva 56 anni e il suo madre, Métheline de BEZIERS-SAUVIAN, aveva 46 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli con Judith de La Tour de Mercoeur. Morì circa 1213, all'età di 80 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Bertrand II de LA TOUR-d'AUVERGNEが約1135年にフランスアウバーグネ-ルhoネ-アルペスプウイ-デ-ドoメラ トウル-ド'アウバーグンで生まれた時，父Bertrand Ier de La Tourは56歳，母Métheline de BEZIERS-SAUVIANは46歳であった。彼はJudith de La Tour de Mercoeurとの間に少なくとも3人の息子をもうけた。彼は約1213年に80歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Bertrand II de LA TOUR-d'AUVERGNE(이)가 대략 1135년 프랑스 오버그니-ho니-알프스 푸이-데-드o 라 토우-드'오버그니에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Bertrand Ier de La Tour(은)는 56세였고, 그의 어머니 Métheline de BEZIERS-SAUVIAN(은)는 46세였다. 그는 Judith de La Tour de Mercoeur(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1213년 80세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Bertrand II de LA TOUR-d'AUVERGNE nasceu aproximadamente 1135, em La Tour-d'Auvergne, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, França, seu pai, Bertrand Ier de La Tour, tinha 56 anos e sua mãe, Métheline de BEZIERS-SAUVIAN, tinha 46 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos com Judith de La Tour de Mercoeur. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1213, com 80 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Bertrand II de LA TOUR-d'AUVERGNE родился около 1135 г. в местности ла тоур-д'аувиргн, пуи-де-дoм, аувиргне-рoне-алпесь, Франция, его отцу по имени Bertrand Ier de La Tour было 56 лет, а матери, которую звали Métheline de BEZIERS-SAUVIAN, 46. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына от Judith de La Tour de Mercoeur. Он умер около 1213 г. в возрасте 80 лет.
